title: Outbound Investment Security Program
summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury has established the Outbound Investment Security Program pursuant to Executive Order (August 9, 2023), prohibiting or requiring notification of certain U.S. investments in advanced technologies in countries of concern. The program covers three categories: semiconductors and microelectronics, quantum information technologies, and artificial intelligence. The final rule implementing the program was issued October 28, 2024, with an effective date of January 2, 2025.
key_points: Executive Order issued August 9, 2023 declared a national emergency to address threats from U.S. investment in sensitive technologies in countries of concern
Countries of concern designated: People's Republic of China, Hong Kong, and Macau
Three technology categories covered: semiconductors/microelectronics, quantum information technologies, and artificial intelligence
Final rule (Outbound Rules) issued October 28, 2024, effective January 2, 2025
Enforcement authority under International Emergency Economic Powers Act (50 U.S.C. §§ 1701 et seq.)
National Interest Exemption available under 31 CFR § 850.502 with multi-agency consultation
why_it_matters: This program represents a significant macro policy intervention restricting capital flows in advanced technology sectors. For investors and market participants, it creates compliance obligations for any outbound investments in covered technologies to China-linked entities. The program could affect valuations of companies involved in these technology supply chains.
macro_context: The program is a macro-level policy intervention that restricts cross-border capital flows in strategic technology sectors. It reflects ongoing U.S.-China technology competition and represents a structural shift in investment restrictions rather than a short-term market catalyst.
